plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for income and broad index stability.
Buy XLE for 5% to diversify into a strong, non-tech setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
This plan aims for capital preservation and low drawdown.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility. Expect smaller gains but also reduced drawdown risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its flagged status and high loss.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it is flagged and in a downtrend.
Buy NVDA for 10% and ERX for 5% to add high-scoring setups.
Keep SPAL at a minimal 1% as a small leveraged exposure.
This plan balances growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality setups. Expect moderate returns with controlled drawdown risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its flagged status and significant loss.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending asset.
Buy NVDA for 15% to concentrate in a top-scoring setup.
Add TECS for 5% and NVDU for 5% to leverage high-scoring setups.
This plan aggressively leans into high-conviction opportunities.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leveraging high-scoring setups. It embraces higher risk and potential for significant drawdown, especially with leveraged ETFs. Daily decay and rebalancing in leveraged products can amplify losses; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD and SMH to 5% each to reduce high-beta exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income focus.
This plan prioritizes steady distributions over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing yield and low drawdown. It significantly reduces growth-oriented assets and leverage. Distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
